channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: US approves $16.5bn arms deal to Gulf states amid rising Iran tensions
item.description: The proposed deal includes the sale of drones, missiles and radars for Gulf allies, including UAE, Kuwait and Jordan.

item.title: Iranians shop for Persian New Year essentials despite war
item.description: Iranians are shopping for essentials to celebrate the beginning of the Persian New Year, Nowruz, despite the war.

item.title: Smoke rises after Iranian missile attack on Israel oil refinery in Haifa
item.description: An Iranian missile struck an oil refinery in the Israel city of Haifa.

item.title: Iran’s strike on Qatar gas facility will reduce supply for 3 to 5 years
item.description: Iran’s strike on Qatar’s Ras Laffan gas facility will cut about 17% of the country’s Liquefied Natural Gas exports.

item.title: Qatar PM on Iran attacks: ‘Wisdom seems to be lacking these days’
item.description: Qatar’s prime minister has said Iran’s claims that it is targeting US bases is “unacceptable and unjustified”.

item.title: Who leads Iran? Assassinations leave leadership and command in question
item.description: Analysts question Tehran’s power dynamics as younger hardliners may rise after killing of leaders Khamenei and Larijani.

item.title: Israel says Haifa oil refinery hit in Iranian missile attack
item.description: Israeli Energy Minister Eli Cohen says 'no significant damage' reported after Iranian strike on Haifa oil refinery.

item.title: Trump references Pearl Harbor during meeting with Japanese PM on Iran war
item.description: US president said that Japan was 'stepping up to the plate' to support the US war without offering details.

item.title: Missile strikes close to reporter during Israeli attacks in Lebanon
item.description: A missile struck metres away from a Russia Today reporter as he was covering Israeli attacks on southern Lebanon.

item.title: Iran attacks cut 17% of Qatar’s LNG capacity for up to 5 years: QatarEnergy
item.description: CEO Saad al-Kaabi says QatarEnergy may have to declare force majeure on long-term contracts for up to five years.
